title: Context Pack Schema
description: Machine-readable, tool-agnostic context envelope for dispatching one bounded task across different agent runtimes while preserving references back to canonical repository context.

schema_version.description: Version of the context-pack contract. 1.1.0 adds the optional `orchestration` block (budget/selection/dropped/determinism); packs that omit `orchestration` remain valid at 1.0.0.

pack_id.description: Stable identifier for this context pack instance.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp at which the pack was assembled.

objective.description: One-sentence description of the task goal.

role.description: Conceptual role that owns this unit of work.

intent_mode.description: Current phase of work for the selected role.

execution_mode.description: Autonomy level applied to this run.

budget_profile.description: Instruction loading profile used when assembling the pack.

approved_scope.description: Explicit scope boundaries for the receiving runtime.

summary.description: Short natural-language summary of what is in scope.

allowed_paths.description: Files, directories, or logical modules allowed for this task.

blocked_paths.description: Paths that should not be modified unless the task is re-approved.

constraints.description: Active rules, approvals, and safety boundaries the run must respect.

source_of_truth.description: Canonical repository references that outrank session memory or adapter-specific instructions.

entrypoint_refs.description: Root entrypoint files such as AGENTS.md.

decision_refs.description: DECISIONS.md entries or equivalent durable decision refs.

contract_refs.description: API, schema, or architecture references that are relevant to the task.

artifacts.description: Task-scoped context material supplied with the pack.

context_files.description: Relevant files or generated artifacts for this task.

critical_context.description: Facts that must not be dropped during compaction or adapter translation.

handoff_artifact_ref.description: Reference to a prior handoff artifact, if this pack was generated from one.

context_anchor.description: Current context-anchor summary for long tasks.

summaries.description: Operational summaries included for continuity. These are informative, not canonical.

project_state.description: Optional live state facts captured at pack assembly time.

expected_output.description: Required response contract for the receiving runtime.

machine_schema_ref.description: Optional schema reference when machine-readable output is required.

success_definition.description: What the adapter should treat as successful completion.

validation_required.description: Whether the runtime is expected to run validation before completion.

trace_id.description: Trace identifier shared across adapters and handoffs.

parent_pack_id.description: Pack ID of the immediate predecessor when chaining packs.

context_version.description: Version of the canonical context set used to assemble this pack.

freshness_cutoff.description: Optional timestamp limiting how stale referenced live state may be.

generated_by.description: Human or agent identity that assembled the pack.

orchestration.description: Optional (schema 1.1.0+) budget-aware orchestration metadata. Records how the pack was selected, ranked, truncated, and made reproducible. Adapter-neutral: the builder reads canonical repo context only (AGENTS.md, rules, project manifest, DECISIONS.md, task-declared files) and does NOT consume IDE or session state.

budget.description: Token budget bookkeeping for this pack.

allocated.description: Token allocation per logical section (e.g., rules, decisions, context_files, critical, reserve). Keys are free-form but should be stable across runs.

estimate_method.description: How the token counts were estimated (e.g., 'word_count*1.35'). Present when the builder estimates tokens itself.

selection.description: Ranked set of refs the builder chose to include. Ordered by priority ascending (1 = most important). Output ordering MUST be deterministic for identical inputs.

ref.description: Path or logical ref that was included.

priority.description: Lower integers are more important. Ties broken deterministically by ref.

score.description: Optional raw relevance score. Present when the builder used a scoring function.

reason.description: Short rationale so humans can audit selection.

truncated.description: True if the ref's content was summarized or cropped before inclusion.

dropped.description: Refs the builder considered but excluded. Empty when no candidates were evaluated.

reason.description: Why the ref was not included (e.g., 'below threshold', 'budget exhausted', 'not in approved scope').

determinism.description: Reproducibility markers. Given the same inputs, a conforming builder MUST produce identical output_hash values.

builder_version.description: Semver of the builder that produced this pack.

input_hash.description: sha256 hex digest of the normalized builder inputs (args + canonical repo files read).

output_hash.description: sha256 hex digest of the canonical-JSON serialization of this pack with the `output_hash` field itself replaced by an empty string.
